campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help local kids afford recreation programs. They also discussed plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including applying for grant funding and reviewing a feasibility study for the project.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth recreation scholarships
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for bridge planning studies
Extension of interim ordinance 2326

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council met to decide how to distribute grant money to local organizations and events. They approved funding for specific groups that applied for grants, set aside money for competitive event grants in the upcoming fiscal year, and made some clarifications to the rules for community events and street closures.
keyTopics: Approved specific groups receiving secured grant funding and their individual dollar amounts
Allocated budget for the Competitive Event Grant Program for the fiscal year
Updated rules and clarifications for Community Event and Street Closure Grants

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This was a San José City Council meeting held on March 25, 2026. The agenda focused on providing accessibility options for residents to watch the meeting, including live translations in over 50 languages and multiple ways to join online or by phone.
keyTopics: Live translation services available in 50+ languages
Multiple viewing options (cable, YouTube, Zoom, phone)
Meeting accessibility for residents unable to attend in person
No public comment period for this particular meeting

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's zoning hearing that was scheduled for March 25, 2026 has been cancelled. The hearing will be rescheduled for Wednesday, April 8, 2026 instead. If you had plans to attend or submit comments about zoning matters, mark your calendar for the new date.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning review process delayed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal funding that helps support affordable housing and community services in Sunnyvale. The commission approved meeting notes from the previous month and heard presentations from organizations seeking grants to help low-income residents.
keyTopics: Federal housing grants available to local organizations
Review of funding applications for affordable housing projects
Community development programs seeking financial support
Approval of previous meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council discussed several initiatives to help local businesses thrive, including a program to help shops get their business licenses in order without penalties, outreach efforts to shopping centers, and plans to encourage residents to shop locally during summer events. The council also explored ways to support small businesses and startups through special incentives and partnerships with private companies to sponsor city events.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping shops catch up on licensing without fines
Shopping center outreach efforts to boost local business participation
Summer Shop Local Campaign to drive customers to Cupertino retailers
Economic incentives for small businesses and startups
Public-private partnerships to fund and sponsor city events

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026, where they postponed several items for further review. The council approved a plan to remove weeds around the city as part of a county-wide program, and they opened up discussion about how the city will spend federal housing grant money in the coming year.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program for 2026
Federal HUD housing grant funding decisions for 2026-27
Multiple agenda items delayed for later consideration

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to handle several routine matters. They recognized March as Women's History Month, approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract with a software company for email security services costing about $93,000 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
